question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the URI.create(sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint()) call in s3Client() when sourceProperties.getUri() is null, by manipulating the SourceType enum endpoint string (e.g., via configuration injection) to include a redirect to an attacker-controlled server that returns stream files with fraudulent transaction fee amounts?
